title: Sign Text Layout
subtitle: Vertical writing, horizontal writing, lanes, centering, and reading levels
summary: Canonical statement of sign text layout for vertical and horizontal writing, including lanes, centering strategy, and SW1-SW4 reading-level defaults.
synopsis: Sign text layout extends the signbox into passages. This entry explains vertical and horizontal writing, lane counts and lane meaning, the 500x500 sign center, centering behavior, punctuation placement, and SW1-SW4 layout defaults.

slides.title: Layout Extends the Signbox
slides.summary: Passages need rules for writing direction, lanes, centering, and text-level spacing.
slides.speech: The signbox preserves the spatial shape of one sign. Sign text layout explains how signs become passages, with vertical and horizontal writing, lane behavior, centering strategy, and reading-level defaults. This is the bridge between the formal sign model and visible text flow.
slides.notes: Use this as the stable technical home for passage-level layout behavior.
